data.title: BRP to buyback up to 3.31M subordinate voting shares
data.description:
• Toronto Stock Exchange has approved BRP (DOOO:CA) renewal of its normal course issuer bid to purchase for cancellation up to 3,331,852 subordinate voting shares (10% of public float) over the twelve-month period ending no later than December 9, 2025.
  • Under the last NCIB that expired on December 4, 2024, the company purchased 3,231,999 subordinate voting shares.
  • The board believes that the purchase by BRP of its subordinate voting shares could represent an appropriate and desirable use of its available cash to increase shareholder value.
  • The shares are higher amid Q3 results.
  • DOOO +12.3% premarket to $54.51
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Genius Group acquires XD Academy, bitcoin learning platform
data.description:
Genius Group (NYSE:GNS), an AI-powered bitcoin education group, acquired XD Academy, a bitcoin and blockchain learning platform, in a move that will help Genius build faculty and courses for the Bitcoin Academy on its Edtech platform, the company said Friday.

The company plans to develop the Bitcoin Academy as part of its AI-powered “Built on Bitcoin” decentralized education network, with on-chain GEMs (Genius Education Merits), AI tutors, and AI Certifications built on the Lightning Network, it said.

Genius (NYSE:GNS) stock fell 2.2% in Friday premarket trading.

Terms of the deal weren't disclosed, as the acquisition isn't material in economic terms, the company said.

Genius Group (GNS) will hold an investor meeting from Bitcoin MENA at 8:30 AM ET.

data.title: GitLab pops as Wall Street praises results, still sees opportunity
data.description:
GitLab (NASDAQ:GTLB) was in the spotlight on Friday after the DevOps company reported stronger-than-expected third-quarter results and guidance, leading to praise from Wall Street.

Shares rose 10% in premarket trading, while Microsoft (MSFT), which owns GitLab's competitor GitHub, was up 0.4%.

"We remain encouraged by GitLab's consolidation wins and large deal activity, and are raising our PT to $80 based on 13x FY26E EV/sales accordingly," KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst Jason Celino wrote in a note to clients.

Celino, who has an Overweight rating on GitLab, said the third-quarter results were driven in part by five deals worth $100,000 or more, along with new customer deals incorporating Duo adoption and several new logos and expansions that included Duo Pro and Enterprise.

"We remain encouraged by GitLab's consolidation opportunities in a fragmented DevSecOps market, and view GitLab's Duo SKUs, Premium tier price increases, and GitLab Dedicated positively as catalysts to potential 30%+ growth in FY25 and FY26...," Celino added.

"Favorite growth idea"

Several other firms raised their price targets on GitLab as well, including Raymond James, Barclays and TD Cowen, with many saying there is still room for the stock to move higher.

RBC Capital Markets analysts, led by Matthew Hedberg, were encouraged by the company's "strong results relative to expectations."

"[We] maintain [our Outperform rating] and raise PT to $80 from $73 as we remain impressed by the market opportunity and strong execution," the analysts wrote. "GTLB remains a favorite growth idea."

They also said Bill Staples taking over GitLab as its new CEO is a "good fit" for the company, given his previous work experience. "We feel that he is a good fit for the role with nearly 30 years of experience with building developer platforms and helping scale large enterprises," they wrote. "Overall, we expect a seamless transition and look forward to hearing more about his strategy and vision over the next few quarters."

Mizuho Securities analyst Gregg Moskowitz also praised the company and said he remained confident in the company's "ability to executive and grow at a high level due to its multiple upside levers including seat expansion, price increases, and upsell potential."

Moskowitz reiterated his Outperform rating on GitLab and upped his price target to $80 from $67 after the results.

data.title: Rubrik soars after strong Q3 'beat and raise,' while analysts keep bullish stance
data.description:
Rubrik's (NYSE:RBRK) stock surged about 22% premarket on Friday after third quarter results and fiscal 2025 guidance beat estimates, and analysts maintained their bullish views on the company.

KeyBanc Capital Markets kept its Overweight rating and raised the price target to $75 from $57.

Analysts led by Eric Heath said that they reiterated the rating after Rubrik's another strong beat and raise on annual recurring revenue, or ARR, and margins. Fiscal third quarter ARR beat by $30M, similar to last quarter and fiscal 2025 ARR guidance raised by the fiscal third quarter beat.

The analysts added that fiscal 2025 subscription contribution margin guidance was raised to -2.5% from -6.5% and -12% in the prior two quarters. Key performance indicators were strong, with Net Revenue Retention, or NRR, again more than 120% and 32% growth in $100K customers.

Momentum in data security was also strong with Data Security Posture Management, or DSPM, customers nearly doubling from last quarter, according to the analysts.

The analysts noted that they remain positive on Rubrik's leadership in backup and recovery and expanding data security platform with significant share gain opportunity in the $11B backup and recovery market with its differentiated focus on cyber resilience, while the increasing severity of ransomware attacks catalyzes enterprises to modernize off legacy solutions that comprise over 40% market share.

Citi raised Rubrik's price target to $75 while noting that a top-to-bottom exceptional fiscal third quarter featured rare-to-see 38% ARR growth year-over-year with underlying New Net ARR, or NNARR, increasing 19% year-over-year (42% beat versus Street), total revenue soared 43% year-over-year (11 points ahead), and operating margin, or OPM, was -13% (+17 points ahead), pushing Subscription-ARR-Contribution Margin at near break-even distance.

The 7% Free cash flow margin also shined, despite well-telegraphed contract/invoicing compression headwinds, according to the analysts.

The analysts noted that robust execution was underpinned by broad-based demand as Rubrik's cyber resiliency message is not only hitting stride, but judging by the fiscal year raise, is inflecting.

The analysts said that this core momentum and credibly growing Gen-Al story, along with reinforced operating expenditure discipline, boosts their confidence in a sustainable, high quality 25% to 30% Subscription ARR trajectory.

Related stocks: CrowdStrike (CRWD), Zscaler (ZS), Fortinet (FTNT) and SentinelOne (S) were largely flat premarket on Friday.

data.title: Realbotix ups cryptocurrency assets by 52% since November 1
data.description:
• Humanoid robots and companionship-based AI company Realbotix (TSXV:XBOT:CA) (OTCQB:XBOTF) said on Friday its cryptocurrency assets have increased by 52% since November 1.
  • The company's cash, cash equivalents and cryptocurrency holdings have an approximate value of $15.3M or C$21.5M as of December 5.
  • The company said it may strategically buy or sell more cryptocurrency holdings depending on its needs and market conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CRH announces interim chief financial officer
data.description:
• CRH plc (NYSE:CRH) has announced that Alan Connolly has been appointed Interim Chief Financial Officer effective January 1, 2025.
  • As announced on September 24, 2024, Jim Mintern, CRH’s current Chief Financial Officer, will assume the role of Chief Executive Officer effective January 1, 2025.
  • The search is ongoing to identify Mr. Mintern’s permanent successor as Chief Financial Officer.
  • Mr. Connolly, a Chartered Accountant, has over three decades of finance experience working at CRH and has held several senior finance roles across the Company’s European and Americas businesses.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BTC Digital falls sharply on pricing of $20M units offering
data.description:
• BTC Digital (NASDAQ:BTCT) has priced underwritten public offering of 2.5M ordinary shares and pre-funded warrants for gross proceeds of approximately $20.0 million.
  • The Pre-Funded Warrants will be immediately exercisable and may be exercised at any time until exercised in full. For each Pre-Funded Warrant sold in the offering, the number of Ordinary Shares in the offering will be decreased on a one-for-one basis.
  • Aegis Capital have a 45-day option to purchase additional ordinary shares of up to 15.0% of the number of ordinary shares sold .
  • The net proceeds together with its existing cash will be used for general corporate purposes and working capital.
  • Closing date is December 9, 2024.
  • The stock was up over 784% in the past one-month as crypto backer, Trump won presidential election.
  • BTCT -30% premarket to $10.86
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple, supply chain in focus as KeyBanc weighs in on Black Friday, November data
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) and some members of its supply chain were in focus on Friday as KeyBanc Capital Markets weighed in on several recent data points, including a "slightly positive" November iPhone carrier survey and Black Friday shopping weekend.

Shares of the Tim Cook-led Apple were little changed in premarket trading.

"Our carrier survey indicates iPhone 16 sell-through in November was tracking slightly above store expectations and normal seasonal trends," analysts John Vinh and Brandon Nispel wrote in a note to clients. "Demand for the iPhone 16 Pro/Max remained relatively healthy, but the base models were still weaker. Store inventories increased slightly and remain relatively healthy (under two DOI) and below prior-year levels. Interest in Apple AI was slightly higher but generally remained a non-factor in driving near-term demand."

KeyBanc has an Underweight rating and $200 price target on Apple.

The research firm's First Look Data showed a 2% month-over-month and 5% year-over-year increase for the iPhone, while below seasonal trends, still grew, the analysts noted. And Black Friday saw a 9% year-over-year increase.

That batch of data is seen as "modestly positive" for part of Apple's supply chain, including Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO), Cirrus Logic (CRUS), Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM), Qorvo (QRVO) and Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS), the analysts said.

For Apple itself, the data may be slightly negative the analysts said, as the data showed indexed spending down 6% month-over-month in November, below the three-year average of a 3% increase.

"We're seeing historical trends suggest no iPhone 16 upgrade bump, and don't see any comparability issues with the data considering AAPL launched a new MacBook Pro at a similar date this November as last year," the analysts wrote.

"Overall, our data is modestly negative, and we expect to see [approximately] in-line growth in F4Q24 vs. AAPL's historical averages."
